message: Node started: Consider two chords, each with endpoints in different quadrants. Let the quadrants be labeled 1, 2, 3, 4 in order. For a single chord, identify the possible pairs of quadrants it can connect. For two chords, determine the probability that they intersect, given that their endpoints are chosen uniformly at random within their respective quadrants. Note that an intersection occurs if and only if the endpoints of the two chords alternate around the circle. Calculate the probability P that two chords intersect, accounting for the constraint that endpoints must be in different quadrants.
